title: Stixis fasciculata
taxonomy: Stixis fasciculata is a plant species, and based on its classification, it belongs to the family Stixisaceae within the order Santalales. This plant is part of the Santalales order, which consists of parasitic flowering plants commonly known as sandalwoods or mistletoes. The family Stixisaceae is a small, monogeneric family with Stixis as its only genus. The order Santalales is a group of flowering plants characterized by their parasitic or hemiparasitic nature. Stixis fasciculata, being a part of this family and order, shares these characteristics and is classified accordingly.
uses: Stixis fasciculata, a plant species native to the Himalayan region, is valued for its potential medicinal properties.
